name: As Portas Reservoir Loop
description: This difficult 30.9-mile road cycling route in A Gudiña offers challenging climbs, stupendous mountain views, and passes the Portas Dam.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around A Gudiña traverse a mountainous region in Galicia, characterized by significant elevation gains and diverse terrain. The area features imposing mountains, lush green valleys, and rustic stone villages. Cyclists encounter challenging climbs and scenic vistas, including reservoirs and historical paths like the Camino de Santiago. Best road cycling routes around A Gudiña The most popular road cycling route is Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop, a 57.0 miles (91.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is As Portas Reservoir Loop, a difficult 30.9 miles (49.7 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and views of the Portas Dam. Local road cyclists also love the From Landscape between Seixas and Contim to Mezquita — driving through the Parque Natural de Montesinho, a 27.5 miles (44.2 km) trail leading through diverse scenery and natural park areas, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around A Gudiña is defined by mountainous terrain, lush valleys, and historical paths. The network offers options for moderate to very difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore A Gudiña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around A Gudiña?
A: Road cycling around A Gudiña is characterized by its mountainous terrain, offering significant elevation gains and challenging climbs. You'll encounter diverse landscapes, from lush green valleys and forested areas to rustic stone villages. The region is part of the imposing Galician mountains, providing spectacular views throughout your ride.

Q: Are there routes suitable for less experienced road cyclists in A Gudiña?
A: While many routes in A Gudiña are rated as difficult, there are also moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portela da Canda Pass loop from A Gudiña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that still offers a good challenge without the extreme elevation of some other trails. It's always recommended to check the route details for elevation and distance before you set out.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling routes in the A Gudiña area?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936491294/itinerario-de-monterrei-and-alto-do-fojo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop&lt;/a&gt; stand out. This difficult 57-mile route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous landscapes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936447410/as-portas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Portas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers challenging climbs and views of the Portas Dam.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in A Gudiña?
A: The region boasts diverse natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portela da Canda Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which offers great views and is a significant crossing point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936447410/as-portas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Portas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides views of the Portas Dam. Additionally, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399127028/from-landscape-between-seixas-and-contim-to-mezquita-driving-through-the-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Landscape between Seixas and Contim to Mezquita&lt;/a&gt; pass through areas near the Parque Natural de Montesinho, offering scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, A Gudiña is historically significant as part of the Camino de Santiago. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Canda (Camino de Santiago)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portela da Canda Pass&lt;/a&gt; also holds cultural value with Jacobean sculptures and wayside crosses. The nearby town of Monterrei, which you might pass on longer routes, features a castle overlooking the town.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in A Gudiña?
A: The region's mountainous character suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for road cycling, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, especially on climbs, while winters might bring colder conditions and potentially snow at higher elevations, making some routes more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around A Gudiña are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936491294/itinerario-de-monterrei-and-alto-do-fojo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936447410/as-portas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Portas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portela da Canda Pass loop from A Gudiña&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What are the typical lengths of road cycling routes around A Gudiña?
A: The road cycling routes in A Gudiña vary significantly in length to suit different preferences and fitness levels. You can find routes ranging from around 25 miles (40 km) to over 55 miles (90 km). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399127028/from-landscape-between-seixas-and-contim-to-mezquita-driving-through-the-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Landscape between Seixas and Contim to Mezquita&lt;/a&gt; is about 27.5 miles, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936491294/itinerario-de-monterrei-and-alto-do-fojo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de Monterrei and Alto do Fojo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 57 miles long.

Q: Can I find places to rest or get refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs are not detailed for every route, the region features rustic stone villages and towns like A Gudiña itself, which offer amenities. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote routes. Some areas also have natural water sources, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1127193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Capela Spring&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6000402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Spring, Washhouse and Pool of Campobecerros&lt;/a&gt;, which can provide refreshing breaks.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the starting points of road cycling routes?
A: A Gudiña is a significant entry point into Galicia, particularly for pilgrims on the Camino de Santiago, suggesting it has some public transport connections. However, for specific route starting points, it's best to research local bus services or consider driving to the trailhead. Parking is generally available in towns and villages, but specific trailhead parking should be checked beforehand.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in A Gudiña?
A: Road cyclists who have explored A Gudiña often praise the region's challenging climbs and the spectacular, diverse scenery. The combination of imposing mountains, lush valleys, and historical paths like the Camino de Santiago provides a unique and rewarding experience. Many appreciate the feeling of traversing a less-traveled, authentic Galician landscape.

Ascent OU-CV-346 from the Conso to O Serro Hydroelectric Power Station
Viana do Bolo is an old trading post located in the mountains of southeastern Galicia

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for the A Gudiña Return of lakes route?
faqs.answer: This is categorized as a "difficult" racebike route, demanding a very good level of fitness. It involves significant elevation gain over its 49.7-kilometer distance, making it challenging for less experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, typical for racebiking. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved and more challenging to navigate. The area is mountainous, with undulating terrain and notable ascents and descents, often exceeding 1,000 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: What kind of views and natural features can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can anticipate stupendous views of the valleys and surrounding mountains. The route specifically passes by the As Portas Reservoir. The broader region offers diverse panoramas, including enchanted forested areas and quaint rustic stone villages, characteristic of southern Galicia.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several notable points. You'll pass by the Portas Dam, experience the challenging Ascent of the OU-CV-346, and enjoy panoramic vistas from the Viewpoint of Viana do Bolo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the A Gudiña Return of lakes route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in A Gudiña. You should be able to find suitable parking within the town to begin your ride. Specific trailhead parking information is not detailed, but A Gudiña serves as the primary access point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, given the mountainous terrain and potential for unpaved sections, cycling during spring or autumn would likely offer the most pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures or adverse weather in the mountains.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other trails. Notably, it crosses parts of the Camino Sanabrés (East) and the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste). You'll also find smaller overlaps with trails like [CIMA OU10] Alto de Cepedelo and Camiño Xabrés por Verín.

faqs.question: Is the A Gudiña Return of lakes route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebiking on roads, which typically isn't suitable for dogs. While there are no explicit restrictions, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on a demanding road cycling route of this nature due to traffic and the physical demands on the animal.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route, especially if it's near Parque Natural do Invernadeiro?
faqs.answer: The A Gudiña Return of lakes route itself does not require a specific permit or entrance fee. While it is in the general vicinity of Parque Natural do Invernadeiro, this specific cycling route does not enter the protected park area directly. If you plan to visit the park separately, you should check their official website for any specific access rules or permits.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a racebike tour on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and mountainous nature, you should bring a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling gear, plenty of water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Weather can change quickly in the mountains, so layers are advisable. A helmet is essential for safety.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in A Gudiña, where you will find amenities like cafes and shops. However, the route itself traverses a more secluded, mountainous region. It's advisable to carry sufficient supplies for the duration of your ride, as options for resupply directly on the trail may be limited.
